summary: Large closed, unmerged WIP/non-matching fighter decomp branch. It added a new src/melee/ft/fighter.c replacing asm/melee/ft/fighter.s, expanded fighter-related structs and prototypes, renamed func_800693AC call sites to Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, and propagated new Fighter field names through fighter-adjacent code. The author explicitly said it was not expected to merge and was intended to preserve work before the fighter struct diverged further from master.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/fighter.c as a 3373-line C translation of fighter.s and deleted asm/melee/ft/fighter.s from the build slice.
Added fighter.c to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED via e_files.mk and added per-object Makefile flags for fighter.c.o, including CFLAGS += -inline noauto and CC_EPI := $(CC).

Expanded src/melee/ft/fighter.h substantially: added includes for functions.h, player.h, stage.h, math.h, gobjuserdata.h, gobjgxlink.h, jobj.h, random.h, controller.h; added RGBA, temporary fighter-related structs, more ftCommonData offsets, nested Fighter damage and callback structs, and many offset-annotated fields.
Renamed the widely-called action-state-change function from func_800693AC to Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC in headers, fighter-adjacent C files, and many asm call sites across character files.
Typed many previously generic declarations in include/functions.h, especially HSD_JObj/HSD_GObj, Vec, Fighter, CollData, and return-type fixes such as f32 or s32 instead of void.

Propagated renamed Fighter fields into existing C files, for example fp->dmg.x1830_percent, fp->x5E8_fighterBones[i].x0_jobj, fp->x618_player_id, fp->x61A_controller_index, p_ftCommonData, and explicit bitfield names such as x2229_b3.
Changed tools/frank.py to pass through vanilla objects when the code-size magic is absent and added a temporary byte-level fix to swap lwz/mr instruction order near function ends.
smart_moves: The PR preserved a large, otherwise diverging fighter.c and Fighter struct reconstruction even though the author did not expect it to merge; this created a reference slice for later targeted decomp work.
Using one semantic name, Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, across C and asm made a high-traffic function searchable and made later action-state work easier to recognize.
The Fighter struct expansion kept original offsets in field comments, which is valuable for validating accesses against assembly and for later incremental struct refinement.
Grouping damage fields under fp->dmg and callbacks under fp->cb made related offset ranges more coherent than a flat list of anonymous filler fields.
The branch recorded decomp.me scratch links and match status comments such as Matches, Fake Match, and No Match, giving future contributors provenance for matching attempts.
Adding -inline noauto specifically for fighter.c.o was a targeted compiler-control tactic rather than changing global build behavior.
decomp_lessons: For huge core structs like Fighter, divergent WIP can become difficult to merge; isolate small, reviewable struct-offset confirmations and symbol renames where possible.
Offset comments are essential when replacing filler with named fields, especially in a struct with bitfields, nested structs, arrays, and damage/callback subregions.
When renaming a hot function from func_XXXXXXXX, update both C prototypes and asm call-site labels so mixed C/asm builds stay linkable and searchable.
Non-matching branches can still be useful as research artifacts, but they should be treated as evidence to mine rather than as directly mergeable code.
Compiler flags like -inline noauto may help one translation unit but should be documented because they become part of the matching context.
Tooling hacks that rewrite bytes, such as the temporary frank.py lwz/mr swap, should be treated cautiously and replaced by source-level or compiler-context fixes when possible.
naming_conventions: Semantic prefix used for fighter core routine: Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC.
Fighter fields generally retained offset-prefixed names, e.g. x1830_percent, x195c_hitlag_frames, x2064_ledgeCooldown, while adding meaning after the offset.
Nested Fighter damage fields were accessed as fp->dmg.xNNNN_name, e.g. fp->dmg.x1830_percent and fp->dmg.x1850_forceApplied.
Callback fields used x21XX_callback_Name inside a cb grouping, e.g. x219C_callback_IASA and x21E0_callback_OnDeath.
JObj-related fields were clarified from generic joint pointers to HSD_JObj names, e.g. FighterBone x0_jobj and x4_jobj2.
Common fighter data pointer was renamed/used as p_ftCommonData instead of raw lbl_804D6554-style global access in touched C files.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the large fighter.s assembly file and introduced fighter.c as the replacement translation unit, but the PR remained explicitly non-matching.
Kept numerous asm files while renaming branch labels/calls to Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, indicating mixed C/asm linkage during incremental decomp.
Used Makefile per-object CFLAGS += -inline noauto for both build and epilogue fighter.c.o.
Used EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED to include src/melee/ft/fighter.c.o in epilogue builds.
Recorded decomp.me scratch URLs and comments documenting match/fake-match/no-match attempts inside fighter.c.
Modified frank.py to skip modification for no-code objects and added a temporary byte sequence pass to swap lwz and mr instruction order, explicitly labeled by the author as a temp fix.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR dump.
The only human rationale is the author body: the branch was unfinished, non-matching, and not expected to merge, but intended to keep fighter.c and Fighter struct work from being ignored.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that merged parts of src/melee/ft/fighter.c or reused Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC.
Search for later changes to Fighter.dmg field grouping and compare against this PR's offset names.
Check whether the p_ftCommonData rename and ftCommonData offsets survived in later master.
Audit whether the tools/frank.py temporary lwz/mr swap was removed or replaced by a safer matching tactic.
Look for later review/merge decisions around using -inline noauto for fighter.c.
